diagnosis: Layer 2 (configuration/runtime contract) failure. The training process itself succeeded (exit code 0, empty STDERR, full train/eval completed), so this is not infrastructure or a code crash. The root cause is an output artifact path contract mismatch introduced in launch wiring: `run_train.slurm` forces `NANORESEARCH_RESULTS_DIR` to a workspace-level path (`.../workspaces/attempt-01/results`) while the job runs from `.../experiment`, and downstream validators typically look in the run-local results location. This can mark the job FAILED despite successful training because expected artifacts are not found where the runner/checker expects them.

patches.description: Adds filesystem copy support to mirror artifacts into optional legacy results path.
Introduces a safe artifact mirroring helper so outputs are available in both primary and legacy result paths.

patches.description: Ensures eval-only runs also publish artifacts to the legacy path expected by some validators.
Mirrors final training artifacts after completion so post-run checks can find metrics/predictions regardless of which results root they inspect.
